title: Pleurothallis bicristata
taxonomy: Pleurothallis bicristata is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Pleurothallis is a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other orchids. Further classification may include its tribe, such as the tribe Cymbidieae.

intro: Pleurothallis bicristata, also known as the two-crested Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis bicristata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
